findings.title: Contribution volume (12 months)
findings.evidence: 26,429 contributions in the last 12 months (13,353 commits, 2,268 pull requests) — about 72 shipped units of work every single day
findings.source: GitHub Octoverse 2023 (100M+ accounts, majority dormant per year); public country leaderboards (gayanvoice/top-github-users) where national top-1000 entry sits in the low five figures of yearly contributions

findings.title: Integration throughput
findings.evidence: 2,268 pull requests opened in 12 months — ~43 reviewed, mergeable units of work every week
findings.source: Octoverse pull-request volume statistics; median active developer opens well under 100 PRs/year

findings.title: Tenure
findings.evidence: On GitHub since 2016 — 9.5 years of shipped history
findings.source: Account creation date (no percentile claimed for tenure)

findings.title: Repository portfolio
findings.evidence: 3 original (non-fork) repositories owned across 0 operated organizations plus the personal account — an institutional footprint
findings.source: GitHub Octoverse account totals vs. repository totals: the median account owns 0–2 public repositories; multiple public analyses of the GH Archive corpus put >30 owned repos in the top percentile of accounts

findings.title: Language breadth
findings.evidence: 2 distinct languages each leading at least one shipped repository — led by JavaScript (1), Python (1)
findings.source: Octoverse language reports: the typical developer ships in 1–3 languages; polyglot activity beyond 5 primary languages across owned repos is rare in GH Archive analyses

findings.title: Full-stack coverage
findings.evidence: Shipped repositories lead in 2 distinct stack layers: backend, frontend — frontend through infrastructure in one pair of hands
findings.source: Layer mapping over primary languages of owned non-fork repos; covering 4+ layers with shipped code is the working definition of full-stack, and polyglot shipping at this breadth is a top-percentile behavior (see language_breadth baseline)

findings.title: Federation architecture
findings.evidence: 0 GitHub organizations operated as a single federated system — separation of concerns applied to an entire estate, not just a codebase
findings.source: GitHub organization membership statistics: most accounts belong to 0–1 organizations and *operate* none
